mnemonics.title: BINARY SEARCH STEPS
mnemonics.content: LEFT LESS RIGHT MORE - IF TARGET IS LESS THAN MID, GO LEFT (HIGH = MID - 1); IF GREATER, GO RIGHT (LOW = MID + 1)

mnemonics.title: MID CALCULATION
mnemonics.content: M.I.D. = MINIMUM INDEX DIVIDED - MID = LOW + (HIGH - LOW) / 2 TO AVOID OVERFLOW

mnemonics.title: COMPLEXITY REMINDER
mnemonics.content: LOGS ARE DIVIDERS - O(LOG N) MEANS HALVING REPEATEDLY LIKE FOLDING PAPER

acronyms.expansion: Leftmost index of current search range
acronyms.usage: Starting point of the search interval in Binary Search

acronyms.expansion: Middle index calculated as low + (high - low) / 2
acronyms.usage: The pivot point used to compare with target element

acronyms.expansion: Rightmost index of current search range
acronyms.usage: Ending point of the search interval in Binary Search

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a ruler from index 0 to n-1. Binary Search places MID at center and 'chops off' the half where target cannot exist. The remaining segment gets smaller like cutting a cake in half repeatedly.
Binary Search creates a balanced binary tree - each comparison has two outcomes (go left or right). Depth of tree = log2(n), representing number of comparisons.
Think of guessing a number between 1-100: ask 'Is it >50?' If yes, eliminate 1-50. Ask 'Is it >75?' If yes, eliminate 51-75. Keep halving the range until you find it.

analogies.analogy: Looking for a word in a dictionary - you don't scan page by page; you open near the middle, check if your word is before or after, then repeat in the appropriate half.
Searching for a book in a sorted shelf: look at the middle shelf, if your book comes before, search the left half; if after, search the right half - eliminating half each time.
Classic 1-100 guessing game: pick 50, if too high pick 25, if too low pick 75. Each guess cuts possibilities in half - this is exactly Binary Search.
